quizQuestion:
El libro {___} por Cervantes.
(The book was written by Cervantes.)
sentenceTranslation: The book was written by Cervantes.
grammarPointLabel: ser + past participle passive: fue escrito
explanation: fue escrito — ser passive in preterite (él/ella form). Structure: ser + past participle, agreeing with the subject (libro = masc. sing.). The agent introduced by por: por Cervantes.

quizQuestion:
La carta {___} ayer.
(The letter was sent yesterday.)
grammarPointLabel: ser + past participle passive: fue enviada (fem.)
explanation: fue enviada — ser passive in preterite with feminine agreement. La carta is feminine, so the past participle agrees: enviada (not enviado). This agreement is essential in Spanish passive voice.

quizQuestion:
La ciudad {___} en el siglo XII.
(The city was founded in the 12th century.)
sentenceTranslation: The city was founded in the 12th century.
grammarPointLabel: ser + past participle passive: fue fundada (fem.)
explanation: fue fundada — ser passive (preterite) with feminine agreement. La ciudad is feminine, so: fue fundada. Historical facts often use ser passive to describe completed past actions.

quizQuestion:
La ley {___} por el Parlamento.
(The law was approved by Parliament.)
sentenceTranslation: The law was approved by Parliament.
grammarPointLabel: ser + past participle passive: fue aprobada + por-agent
explanation: fue aprobada — ser passive (preterite) + por-agent. La ley is feminine: aprobada. The agent (Parliament) is introduced by por. This is a complete passive construction with all components.

quizQuestion:
Los documentos {___} por el director.
(The documents were signed by the director.)
sentenceTranslation: The documents were signed by the director.
grammarPointLabel: ser + past participle passive: fueron firmados (masc. pl.)
explanation: fueron firmados — ser passive (preterite) with masculine plural agreement. Los documentos is masculine plural: firmados. Fueron = preterite of ser (ellos/ellas).

quizQuestion:
La novela {___} a veinte idiomas.
(The novel was translated into twenty languages.)
sentenceTranslation: The novel was translated into twenty languages.
grammarPointLabel: ser + past participle passive: fue traducida (fem.)
explanation: fue traducida — ser passive (preterite). La novela is feminine: traducida. Note: passive + a + number of languages is a common pattern in formal Spanish.

quizQuestion:
El edificio {___} por arquitectos famosos.
(The building was built by famous architects.)
sentenceTranslation: The building was built by famous architects.
grammarPointLabel: ser + past participle passive: fue construido (masc.)
explanation: fue construido — ser passive (preterite). El edificio is masculine: construido. The agent los arquitectos famosos is introduced by por.

quizQuestion:
{___} cinco idiomas en esa empresa.
(Five languages are spoken in that company.)
sentenceTranslation: Five languages are spoken in that company.
grammarPointLabel: se-passive: se hablan (impersonal passive — plural subject)
explanation: Se hablan — se-passive (pasiva refleja). When the agent is unknown or unimportant, use se + verb. The verb agrees with the subject (cinco idiomas = plural → hablan). This is more natural than the ser passive in everyday speech.

quizQuestion:
{___} pisos en este edificio.
(Apartments are sold in this building.)
sentenceTranslation: Apartments are sold in this building.
explanation: Se venden — se-passive. Pisos is plural → verb is plural (venden). The se-passive is commonly used in notices, signs, and announcements. Compare: se vende un piso (singular).

quizQuestion:
{___} tres puentes el año pasado.
(Three bridges were built last year.)
sentenceTranslation: Three bridges were built last year.
explanation: Se construyeron — se-passive in preterite. Tres puentes = plural → construyeron (preterite plural). The se-passive can appear in any tense, agreeing with the noun subject.

quizQuestion:
{___} varios experimentos durante el estudio.
(Several experiments were carried out during the study.)
sentenceTranslation: Several experiments were carried out during the study.
explanation: Se realizaron — se-passive in preterite. Varios experimentos = plural → realizaron. The se-passive is common in academic and formal writing to avoid naming the agent.

quizQuestion:
La puerta {___} — no podemos entrar.
(The door is closed — we can't enter.)
sentenceTranslation: The door is closed — we can't enter.
grammarPointLabel: estar + past participle: está cerrada (resultative state)
explanation: está cerrada — estar + past participle describes a resultative state (the current condition resulting from a past action). Contrast: la puerta fue cerrada (ser passive = the action of closing) vs. la puerta está cerrada (estar = the current state).

quizQuestion:
El trabajo {___} — podemos irnos.
(The work is finished — we can leave.)
sentenceTranslation: The work is finished — we can leave.
grammarPointLabel: estar + past participle: está terminado (masc. — resultative)
explanation: está terminado — estar + past participle. El trabajo is masculine: terminado. This describes the current state of completion, not the action of finishing (which would use ser).

quizQuestion:
El contrato {___} — ya es oficial.
(The contract is signed — it's now official.)
sentenceTranslation: The contract is signed — it's now official.
grammarPointLabel: estar + past participle: está firmado (resultative state)
explanation: está firmado — estar + past participle. Describes the present state after signing. El contrato is masculine: firmado. This is distinct from fue firmado (the action of signing happened).

quizQuestion:
La mesa {___} para la cena.
(The table is set for dinner.)
sentenceTranslation: The table is set for dinner.
grammarPointLabel: estar + past participle: está puesta — irregular (poner)
explanation: está puesta — estar + past participle of poner (irregular: puesto/puesta). La mesa is feminine: puesta. The table is currently in the state of being set, resulting from the action of setting it.
